title: Add OpenClaw cron-trigger scheduler mode for mission-control pipeline runs
summary: Introduced `SCHEDULER_MODE=openclaw-cron`, a token-protected external trigger endpoint, and runtime mode introspection so OpenClaw cron can drive pipeline starts while Mission Control retains DAG execution and governance.
changes: Added scheduler mode switch (`internal` vs `openclaw-cron`) in mission-control runtime startup.
Added `GET /api/scheduler/mode` endpoint for runtime scheduler-mode visibility.
Added `POST /api/jobs/:id/trigger` endpoint that starts runs with `trigger=scheduler`.
Added optional trigger token enforcement via `MISSION_CONTROL_CRON_TRIGGER_TOKEN` and `x-mc-cron-token` header.
Added PI helper script to wire OpenClaw cron jobs to Mission Control trigger endpoint.
Extended mission-control API tests to validate mode endpoint and trigger token guard.
risks: External cron flow depends on OpenClaw cron reliability and correct token configuration.
If both internal and external schedulers are enabled operationally, duplicate runs are possible.
rollback: Set `SCHEDULER_MODE=internal` to re-enable built-in interval scheduler.
Stop using `/api/jobs/:id/trigger` and remove cron jobs created by `scripts/pi/openclaw-cron-wire.sh`.
